_note: Per-tile detail for Areas of Expertise (skill/spec/experience). how = how to build it, show = how you show it. Surfaced in the node panel and in My Path Next Steps.

charge.how: Ask to take charge on a quiet shift, then a busy one. Learn the assignment sheet before you have to own it.
charge.show: You make the assignment, run the board, and the unit holds together on a short-staffed night.

corr.how: Take a contract or per-diem block inside a correctional facility. Learn the security rules cold before the clinical ones.
corr.show: You deliver real care inside the constraints, without losing the patient in the custody.

digital.how: Join a health-tech startup or a pilot team. Trade the protocol for shipping and learn the regs as they bite.
digital.show: You move a product from idea to in-use, and you can speak both clinician and engineer.

disaster.how: Run the drills, get on the emergency-management committee, take ICS and NIMS training before the surge is real.
disaster.show: When the surge hits, you triage at scale and the normal rules bend without breaking you.

float.how: Volunteer for the float pool. Learn each unit's supply room and charge nurse fast.
float.show: You walk onto any unit and are competent by the second hour.

global.how: Sign on with a humanitarian or mission org for a defined trip. Pack light, plan for no resources.
global.show: You improvise care with what you brought and prioritize when you cannot do everything.

magnet.how: Work a Magnet-designated unit and join a shared-governance council.
magnet.show: You bring a bedside problem to governance and leave with a changed practice.

military.how: Serve, or take a VA or military post. Learn the system on top of the medicine.
military.show: You handle the trauma and the bureaucracy both, mission first.

night.how: Take a stretch of nights on purpose. Build the autonomy and protect your sleep like a job.
night.show: You run a floor with half the staff and the doctor a phone call away.

rapid.how: Get on the rapid response or code team. Run the mock codes until the algorithm is muscle memory.
rapid.show: You walk into a room going bad and the room gets calmer because you did.

research-site.how: Work a unit that runs trials. Learn consent and protocol fidelity as clinical skills.
research-site.show: You deliver protocol care that holds up to an audit and never cut a consent corner.

stepdown.how: Take a step-down or progressive-care line. Learn the drips and the early-warning signs.
stepdown.show: You catch the step-down patient sliding toward the ICU before the numbers scream.

flight.how: Build ICU time, earn your CCRN or CFRN, then apply to a transport team.
flight.show: You run critical care alone at altitude with only what you packed.

trauma.how: Work a designated trauma center and learn the activation roles.
trauma.show: You hold your spot in organized chaos and the protocol runs through you.

travel.how: Take a travel contract once you are solid. Learn to onboard yourself in a day.
travel.show: You drop into a strange unit, find the supply room, and are billable by lunch.

ms.how: Start on med-surg and take the full load. Five-plus patients, every system.
ms.show: You juggle five acute patients and still catch the one quietly going bad.

bari.how: Work a unit with a bariatric program. Learn the equipment and check your own bias.
bari.show: You give equipment-ready, dignity-first care the rest of the system skips.

bh-pop.how: Take a behavioral-health rotation. Learn de-escalation and safety before meds.
bh-pop.show: You hold a unit through a crisis without it becoming a code gray.

hospice-pop.how: Spend time on hospice or palliative care. Learn presence over fixing.
hospice-pop.show: You sit with a dying patient and a scared family and make the last chapter better.

geri.how: Work geriatrics or long-term care. Learn polypharmacy, falls, and goals of care.
geri.show: You spot the drug interaction and steer the family toward what actually matters.

underserved.how: Work a safety-net clinic or shelter. Care with no net under it.
underserved.show: You deliver real care without the resources, and you do not look away.

neo.how: Take a newborn nursery or NICU step. Learn the first-hours assessment.
neo.show: You read a newborn in the first minutes and act before the parents know.

onc-pop.how: Work an oncology unit. Walk the cure-to-comfort arc with patients.
onc-pop.show: You manage the chemo and the conversation, start to finish, without flinching.

peds-pop.how: Take a peds rotation. Learn that you treat the parent too.
peds-pop.show: You read the child by age and bring the parent along at every step.

substance.how: Work a unit with MAT and detox. Drop the judgment and learn withdrawal cold.
substance.show: You manage withdrawal and meet the patient straight, opinions aside.

va.how: Take a VA post. Learn service-connected conditions and the system's own rules.
va.show: You navigate the VA and give an owed population the care it earned.

womens.how: Work women's health or an OB unit. Learn the care the system shortchanges.
womens.show: You deliver reproductive and prenatal care that closes a gap instead of widening it.

amc.how: Take a post at a teaching hospital. Learn to teach and to navigate the residents.
amc.show: You handle the rare case and the bureaucracy in the same shift.

amb.how: Work an outpatient clinic. Learn prevention and the chronic-disease rhythm.
amb.show: You keep a panel of chronic patients out of the hospital.

asc.how: Work an ambulatory surgery center. Learn the lean, fast turnover.
asc.show: You turn a room and keep the schedule without cutting safety.

fqhc.how: Work a community health center. Do a lot with a little, for everyone.
fqhc.show: You stretch thin resources across a panel that has nowhere else to go.

comm.how: Work a community hospital. Be the broad-scope resource.
comm.show: You are the person the floor asks, across every kind of patient.

rural.how: Take a critical-access or rural post. Be a generalist by necessity.
rural.show: You stabilize anything that comes in and arrange the long transfer.

dialysis-set.how: Work a dialysis center. Learn the routine and the long relationship.
dialysis-set.show: You know your chair patients for years and catch their changes early.

irf.how: Work inpatient rehab. Learn the three-hour therapy day and the function goals.
irf.show: You drive the daily wins that get a patient back to their life.

ltac.how: Work an LTAC. Learn vents and complex wounds on the long stay.
ltac.show: You manage the patient too sick for a SNF and too stable for the ICU.

ltc.how: Work long-term care. Build the relational, chronic side.
ltc.show: You hold the long relationship the rest of the system forgets.

office.how: Work a physician office. Learn the panel and the long relationship.
office.show: You know the family and the history and catch the change over years.

retail.how: Work a retail or pharmacy clinic. Learn fast, convenient, protocol care.
retail.show: You handle vaccines and minor illness and refer the thing that needs more.

snf.how: Work a skilled nursing facility. Learn where hospital discharge actually lands.
snf.show: You run post-acute rehab and the long stay without losing the person.

virt.how: Take telehealth shifts. Learn to assess through a screen and spot the new failure modes.
virt.show: You triage and follow up by video and know when to send them in.

urgent.how: Work urgent care. Build the suture-strep-x-ray bread and butter.
urgent.show: You handle the middle ground and route the emergency without missing it.

airway.how: Practice positioning, suction, and adjuncts on the manikin, then assist real intubations.
airway.show: You open and hold an airway and have the right adjunct in hand before it is asked for.

transfuse.how: Run the two-person check and the reaction protocol until both are automatic.
transfuse.show: You hang blood, catch a reaction early, and your documentation is airtight.

cline.how: Learn the dressing change and access protocol, and own your unit's CLABSI bundle.
cline.show: Your central lines stay clean and CLABSI stays off your unit.

chest.how: Set up the drainage system under supervision and learn the water seal cold.
chest.show: You manage a chest tube and know normal from an emergency.

acls.how: Get ACLS-certified and run the mock codes until the algorithm is reflex.
acls.show: You stay calm in a code and the rhythm reads itself to you.

falls.how: Round on purpose, use the bed alarm, and learn which patient will try to stand.
falls.show: Your falls go down because you saw the one who was going to get up.

ppe.how: Drill donning and doffing until you never contaminate yourself.
ppe.show: You break isolation cleanly every time, even tired.

iv.how: Get reps on easy sticks, then the hard ones, then central line care.
iv.show: You get the line the others gave up on, and you are useful on any floor.

meds.how: Lock in the five rights and learn high-alert drugs and titration cold.
meds.show: Your med passes are clean and you catch the order that was wrong.

ngt.how: Learn placement, confirmation, and feeds, with aspiration always in mind.
ngt.show: You place and confirm a tube and keep the feed out of the lungs.

assess.how: Run a full head-to-toe every shift until you drop the checklist. Read the trend, not the number.
assess.show: You catch the decline before the monitor alarms and your handoff names what to watch.

phleb.how: Get reps until you draw clean without blowing the vein.
phleb.show: You get the draw on the first stick, even the hard arm.

restraints.how: Learn least-restrictive options and the check-and-document cadence.
restraints.show: You keep a patient safe without losing their dignity or the paperwork.

sterile.how: Practice setting and holding a field until breaking it feels wrong.
sterile.show: Your field stays sterile and the procedure stays clean.

tele.how: Learn the rhythms cold and watch real strips until the dangerous ones jump out.
tele.show: You read the strip and know when it actually matters.

trach.how: Learn suctioning and tie changes on the manikin, then at the bedside.
trach.show: You manage the trach and protect the airway someone is breathing through.

foley.how: Learn sterile insertion and sizing, with CAUTI always in mind.
foley.show: You place a Foley clean and pull it the moment it is not needed.

vent.how: Learn the modes and alarms with RT, then troubleshoot real ones.
vent.show: You read a vent, fix the alarm, and know weaning from worsening.

vitals.how: Stop charting numbers and start charting trends. Ask what each change is telling you.
vitals.show: You flag the creeping respiratory rate before anyone else sees it.

wound.how: Learn staging and dressings, and shadow the wound nurse.
wound.show: You stage the wound right, dress it well, and escalate at the right moment.

coord.how: Own a complex discharge end to end. Track every handoff.
coord.show: You move a patient through a fragmented system without dropping the ball.

deesc.how: Take a de-escalation course and practice tone and space on the floor.
deesc.show: You talk someone down before it becomes a code gray.

goc.how: Sit in on goals-of-care talks, then lead one with a mentor in the room.
goc.show: You run the hard conversation honestly, unhurried, on the patient's terms.

sbar.how: Use SBAR on every handoff until it is the only way you report.
sbar.show: Your 30-second report leaves the next shift knowing exactly what to watch.

literacy.how: Learn to run an interpreter right and write at a sixth-grade level.
literacy.show: Your patient teaches the plan back to you in their own words.

mi.how: Train in motivational interviewing and practice change talk over lecturing.
mi.show: Your patient names their own reason to change instead of nodding at yours.

edu.how: Use teach-back on every discharge and meet people where they are.
edu.show: Your patient can repeat the plan and actually does it at home.

present.how: Volunteer to present at staff ed or rounds, then to leadership.
present.show: You stand in front of the room and the point lands.

abg.how: Work a stepwise method on real gases until acid-base reads fast.
abg.show: You call the acid-base picture and what the lungs and kidneys are doing about it.

ekg.how: Drill 12-leads until the STEMI and the artifact separate instantly.
ekg.show: You catch the STEMI on the strip before the machine prints its guess.

radread.how: Learn the systematic read and always compare to the radiologist's report.
radread.show: You know what to escalate off a film without reading like a radiologist.

labint.how: Learn the panels and which value is the emergency versus the recheck.
labint.show: You see the critical lab and act before the call comes.

poct.how: Get trained and competency-checked on each bedside device.
poct.show: You run the bedside test right and trust the number you act on.

pocus.how: Take a POCUS course and log scans with a credentialed reviewer.
pocus.show: You get the fast look or the line without waiting on radiology.

pft.how: Learn spirometry and lung volumes, and coach the effort that makes them valid.
pft.show: You run a PFT that is technically clean and read the pattern.

cerner.how: Get hands on a Cerner or Oracle Health build or training domain.
cerner.show: You navigate and build in Cerner, not just chart in it.

cds.how: Build an alert or order set and watch whether clinicians use it or click past.
cds.show: Your decision support changes care instead of adding a click.

infx.how: Get on an informatics project. Sit between the clinician and the build.
infx.show: You translate a bedside problem into a workflow the system supports.

bi.how: Build a dashboard in Tableau or Power BI from a real data pull.
bi.show: A leader reads your dashboard and makes a decision from it.

data.how: Pull a report, find the trend, and write the true story in plain language.
data.show: Your analysis survives scrutiny and changes what someone does.

ehr.how: Master order navigation and charting that holds up, without drowning in clicks.
ehr.show: Your chart tells the story and stands up to an audit.

epic.how: Get Epic-certified or onto a Chronicles build team.
epic.show: You build the workflow where clinical reality meets the database.

interop.how: Learn HL7 and FHIR basics and trace one interface end to end.
interop.show: You find the break in the plumbing when two systems stop talking.

coding.how: Learn ICD-10 and CPT and code real charts against an auditor.
coding.show: Your codes are accurate and the claim does not bounce.

hipaa.how: Learn access, audit, and breach response before you need them.
hipaa.show: You handle the record right and would pass the audit cold.

sql.how: Learn enough SQL to pull your own data instead of waiting on a report.
sql.show: You answer your own question with a query the same day.

budget.how: Learn your cost center and productivity numbers and defend your FTEs.
budget.show: You speak budget to leadership and keep your staffing.

change.how: Lead one adoption rollout and plan for the humans, not just the tech.
change.show: Your change sticks because people chose it, not just got it.

lean.how: Take Lean or Six Sigma training and map one real value stream.
lean.show: You cut waste from a process and the front line feels it.

policy.how: Write or revise one unit policy against the actual regs.
policy.show: Your policy keeps the unit on the right side of survey.

precept.how: Precept a new hire end to end and own their first weeks.
precept.show: The unit runs on people you trained.

pm.how: Run one project with a real scope and timeline. Get a PMP if you go formal.
pm.show: Your rollout lands on time and people use it.

qi.how: Run a PDSA cycle on a real problem and chase root cause, not blame.
qi.show: You fix the process and the metric moves.

staffing.how: Own the schedule for a stretch and match bodies to acuity.
staffing.show: You staff to the acuity and the floor is covered when it counts.

lead.how: Take charge on hard nights and hold the unit together.
lead.show: People follow your lead when it is short-staffed and going sideways.

stats.how: Learn enough biostatistics to judge whether a result means anything.
stats.show: You read a study and know if its result holds up.

ebp.how: Take one practice and rebuild it from the literature, not habit.
ebp.show: You replace how it has always been done with what the evidence says.

research.how: Join a study team and learn IRB and subject protection.
research.show: You run a protocol that protects subjects and survives the IRB.

apheresis.how: Train on the apheresis circuit for donation and treatment both.
apheresis.show: You run the pull-separate-return safely and catch a reaction early.

cath.how: Build cardiac time, then train in the cath lab on door-to-balloon.
cath.show: You move fast on a STEMI and the door-to-balloon clock stays low.

ep.how: Train in EP on ablations and device checks.
ep.show: You support an ablation and manage the device interrogation.

er.how: Work the ED and build triage as a core reflex.
er.show: You take anything through the door and sort it fast and right.

endo.how: Train in endoscopy on scopes, sedation, and fast room turnover.
endo.show: You run the scope room safely and keep it moving.

infusion.how: Train in infusion on chemo, biologics, and long-term access.
infusion.show: You run the pump and the protocol and protect the access.

ir.how: Build imaging time, then train in interventional radiology.
ir.show: You support the image-guided fix that avoids the OR.

pacu.how: Train in PACU on airway, pain, and the first hour after anesthesia.
pacu.show: You wake patients up safely and catch the airway before it drops.

or.how: Train in the OR on sterile field and the surgical count.
or.show: You hold the field and the count and move with the team's choreography.

wocn.how: Get WOCN-certified and own the non-healing wounds and ostomies.
wocn.show: Your wounds heal that others gave up on, and your ostomy patients live well.

addiction.how: Work addiction treatment and learn MAT and withdrawal without judgment.
addiction.show: You manage MAT and meet the patient straight, every time.

beh.how: Work behavioral health and master de-escalation and safety.
beh.show: You hold the most under-resourced unit together with skill, not luck.

forensic.how: Train as a SANE and learn evidence and the chain of custody.
forensic.show: You care for the patient and preserve the case at the same time.

home.how: Work home health and learn the social determinants up close.
home.show: You deliver real care in the living room, alone, and read the home.

pall.how: Work hospice or palliative care and learn comfort as the goal.
pall.show: You run the hard conversation and give dignity at the end.

occ.how: Work occupational health on injuries, screenings, and return-to-work.
occ.show: You keep workers healthy and on the job and handle the injury when it comes.

publichealth.how: Work public health and think population over patient.
publichealth.show: You move a community metric, not just one patient's.

rehab.how: Work rehabilitation and chase function over cure.
rehab.show: You drive the slow daily wins that get someone back.

school.how: Work school health as the one nurse for a whole building.
school.show: You handle everything from asthma to a broken arm, solo, all day.

telehealth-spec.how: Take telehealth and learn triage and follow-up through a screen.
telehealth-spec.show: You run the visit that did not need a drive and catch what does.

burn.how: Build ICU time, then train in burn on resuscitation and wound care.
burn.show: You manage the fluid resuscitation and the wound at the deepest end.

cicu.how: Build cardiac and ICU time, then train on post-op hearts and pumps.
cicu.show: You live in the hemodynamics and manage the drips and the balloon pump.

ecmo.how: Get strong ICU experience, then complete ECMO specialty training.
ecmo.show: You run the ECMO circuit when it is the last option left.

icu.how: Step up from step-down to the ICU and learn the 1:1 and 1:2 sickest patients.
icu.show: You hold the tightest margins with one or two patients and miss nothing.

nicu.how: Train in the NICU on grams-not-pounds and the newborn rulebook.
nicu.show: You manage the smallest patients where every gram counts.

nccu.how: Train in neuro ICU on strokes, bleeds, and ICP.
nccu.show: Your hourly neuro check catches the change that matters.

picu.how: Train in the PICU on weight-based critical care with families in the room.
picu.show: You run weight-based everything and keep the parents with you.

sicu.how: Build surgical and ICU time, then train on the fresh post-op critical patient.
sicu.show: You manage the unstable surgical patient and the fresh belly.

transplant.how: Train in transplant on immunosuppression and rejection.
transplant.show: You manage pre and post transplant and play the long rejection game.

antepartum.how: Work high-risk OB and learn the long monitored admission.
antepartum.show: You watch the high-risk pregnancy and catch the turn early.

ld.how: Train in labor and delivery and learn the fast route from routine to emergency.
ld.show: You manage two patients at once and move when it goes sideways.

lactation.how: Build OB time and earn the IBCLC for lactation.
lactation.show: Your support decides whether breastfeeding actually works for the family.

peds-spec.how: Train in a pediatric specialty on doses, development, and the parent.
peds-spec.show: You treat the kid as a kid, not a small adult, and bring the parent along.

peds.how: Work pediatrics and learn the different doses, fears, and wins by age.
peds.show: You read the child by age and win the trust of the room.

postpartum.how: Work mother-baby and learn to teach two patients in the first days.
postpartum.show: You manage recovery and feeding and send a confident family home.

card.how: Work cardiology on rhythms and failure, the hospital workhorse.
card.show: You read the rhythm and manage the failure without a consult for every step.

endocrine.how: Work endocrine and learn insulin and the slow chronic management.
endocrine.show: You keep the diabetic out of the hospital with the boring daily management.

gi.how: Work GI on guts, scopes, and livers.
gi.show: You manage the bleed and the scope prep and read the liver numbers.

geri-spec.how: Work geriatric medicine on frailty and what matters late in life.
geri-spec.show: You cut the polypharmacy and steer toward what the patient actually wants.

id.how: Work infectious disease on stewardship and isolation.
id.show: You pick the right drug and contain the bug before it spreads.

neph.how: Work nephrology on fluids and electrolytes and dialysis decisions.
neph.show: You manage the fluids and electrolytes and call the dialysis timing.

dial.how: Work dialysis on access and clearances and the long relationship.
dial.show: You protect the access and know your three-times-a-week patients cold.

onc.how: Work oncology on chemo and the long haul.
onc.show: You manage the chemo and stay present for the long haul.

pain.how: Work pain management on the relief-versus-risk balance.
pain.show: You relieve pain and manage the risk with judgment, not a default.

pulm.how: Work pulmonology on vents and the chronic breather, RT's home turf.
pulm.show: You manage the vent and the chronic lung patient without flinching.

sleep.how: Work sleep medicine on apnea, studies, and CPAP adherence.
sleep.show: You read the study and get the patient to actually wear the CPAP.

pj-healthy.how: Work prevention, primary care, and screening. Learn risk reduction before disease starts.
pj-healthy.show: You move a well patient's risk down and keep them out of the system.

pj-at-risk.how: Work primary care and early intervention. Catch the trajectory before it becomes a diagnosis.
pj-at-risk.show: You bend a prediabetic, prehypertensive trajectory back toward baseline.

pj-acute.how: Work the ED or an acute floor. Learn fast assessment and time-limited problems.
pj-acute.show: You stabilize the acute episode and get the patient back to baseline.

pj-chronic.how: Work chronic-disease management. Learn the slow daily control that keeps people home.
pj-chronic.show: You keep the diabetic or heart-failure patient stable and out of the hospital.

pj-recovery.how: Work rehab, home health, or follow-up. Learn the work of returning to function.
pj-recovery.show: You drive the daily wins that get a patient back toward baseline.

pj-complex.how: Work case management or complex care. Learn the 5% who use most of the system.
pj-complex.show: You hold the multi-morbid patient together across stacking conditions.

pj-critical.how: Build ICU and code-team time. Learn life-threatening, all-hands care.
pj-critical.show: You manage the decompensating patient when it is all hands.

pj-palliative.how: Work hospice or palliative care. Learn comfort as the goal.
pj-palliative.show: You shift the goal to comfort and give dignity at the end.

pj-preconception.how: Work women's health and primary care. Learn the health that precedes pregnancy.
pj-preconception.show: You optimize a patient's health before conception.

pj-pregnant.how: Work prenatal clinics or OB. Learn pregnancy as a monitored state, not an illness.
pj-pregnant.show: You run the prenatal course and catch the deviation early.

pj-highrisk-preg.how: Work MFM or antepartum. Learn the pregnancy that needs watching.
pj-highrisk-preg.show: You manage gestational diabetes, preeclampsia, and the long admission.

pj-labor.how: Train in labor and delivery. Learn the highest-acuity hours of a healthy course.
pj-labor.show: You manage the event and move fast when it turns.

pj-ob-emergency.how: Build L&D time. Learn hemorrhage, eclampsia, and the OB rapid response.
pj-ob-emergency.show: You run the obstetric emergency that maternal mortality hinges on.

pj-postpartum.how: Work mother-baby. Learn the fourth trimester, recovery, and the warning signs.
pj-postpartum.show: You catch postpartum depression and hypertension before they hide.

pj-newborn-healthy.how: Work the newborn nursery. Learn the first breath, feed, and screen.
pj-newborn-healthy.show: You clear a healthy newborn for home with confidence.

pj-atrisk-newborn.how: Work a special-care nursery. Learn jaundice, late-preterm, and poor feeding.
pj-atrisk-newborn.show: You catch the at-risk newborn in a worried first week.

pj-sick-infant.how: Work peds ED or peds floors. Learn infant infection and dehydration.
pj-sick-infant.show: You manage the bounce-back sick infant and read the subtle signs.

pj-chronic-infant.how: Work peds specialty or complex care. Learn congenital and chronic infant needs.
pj-chronic-infant.show: You manage the congenital-heart or feeding-tube infant over the long game.

pj-nicu.how: Train in the NICU. Learn prematurity and respiratory failure in the most fragile.
pj-nicu.show: You manage the most fragile patients in the building.

pj-neonatal-palliative.how: Work NICU palliative care. Learn comfort when cure is not the goal.
pj-neonatal-palliative.show: You support a family through a newborn's comfort care with dignity.
